_placeholderNotice: Enumeration orders follow the two most widely circulated ḥadīth transmissions of the ninety-nine names: Jāmiʿ at-Tirmidhī (no. 3507) and Sunan Ibn Mājah (no. 3861), both narrated from Abū Hurayrah. Per SPEC.md §9, a qualified scholar should confirm the citations and ordering before public release.

enumerations.title: Tirmidhī enumeration
enumerations.source: Jāmi' at-Tirmidhī, Book of Supplications (Da'awāt), hadith no. 3507; narrated on the authority of Abū Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him). The specific list of names is attributed to Al-Walīd ibn Muslim's addition to the hadith text; scholarly discussion of this transmission exists.
enumerations.note: This is among the most widely circulated enumerations in the Muslim world. The hadith itself (that Allah has ninety-nine names and whoever enumerates them enters Paradise) is authenticated in the major collections; the specific list of names was added by a later narrator and its exact form varies across manuscript traditions.

enumerations.title: Ibn Mājah enumeration
enumerations.source: Sunan Ibn Mājah, Book of Supplications (Du'ā'), hadith no. 3861; narrated on the authority of Abū Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him). Like the Tirmidhī narration, the specific list of names is a later addition. Minor differences between the two lists exist and are part of ongoing scholarly discussion.
enumerations.note: The Ibn Mājah narration of the ninety-nine names contains minor differences from the Tirmidhī version. Both transmissions trace through similar chains and the differences reflect variation in the manuscript tradition.
